ddls:
CREATE TABLE "claims" (
  "claim_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each claim */,
  "politician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the politician making the claim */,
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the topic related to the claim */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the claim was made */,
  "claim_text" TEXT /* Text of the claim */,
  "claim_type" TEXT /* Type of claim (e.g., statement, quote, data point) */,
  "accuracy_rating" TEXT /* Accuracy rating of the claim (e.g., true, false, half-true) */,
  "context" TEXT /* Context or background information related to the claim */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the claim was made */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the primary source of the claim (foreign key to the sources table) */,
  "evidence" TEXT /* Text or links to evidence supporting or refuting the claim */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the claim was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("claim_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_claims_politician_id FOREIGN KEY ("politician_id") REFERENCES politicians ("politician_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_claims_topic_id FOREIGN KEY ("topic_id") REFERENCES topics ("topic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_claims_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "politicians" (
  "politician_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each politician */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the politician */,
  "party" TEXT /* Party affiliation of the politician */,
  "office" TEXT /* Office held by the politician */,
  "state" TEXT /* State or region the politician represents */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Brief biography of the politician */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the politician */,
  "social_media" TEXT /* Social media handles or links for the politician */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the politician's information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("politician_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each topic */,
  "topic_name" TEXT /* Name of the topic */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the topic */,
  "parent_topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent topic (to support hierarchical topics) */,
  "importance" INTEGER /* Numerical score indicating the importance of the topic */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the topic was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "claim_id" INTEGER /* ID of the claim the source is related to */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of source (e.g., news article, social media post) */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the source */,
  "source_text" TEXT /* Text of the source */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the source */,
  "publisher" TEXT /* Publisher or platform of the source */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Date the source was published */,
  "reliability_score" INTEGER /* Numerical score indicating the reliability of the source */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the source was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sources_claim_id FOREIGN KEY ("claim_id") REFERENCES claims ("claim_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fact_checkers" (
  "fact_checker_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fact-checker */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the fact-checker */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the fact-checker belongs to */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Brief biography of the fact-checker */,
  "expertise" TEXT /* Areas of expertise of the fact-checker */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Numerical score indicating the reliability or accuracy of the fact-checker's work */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the fact-checker's information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fact_checker_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "accuracy_ratings" (
  "accuracy_rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each accuracy rating */,
  "claim_id" INTEGER /* ID of the claim being rated */,
  "fact_checker_id" INTEGER /* ID of the fact-checker assigning the rating */,
  "rating" TEXT /* Accuracy rating assigned to the claim (e.g., true, false, half-true) */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the rating was assigned */,
  "evidence" TEXT /* Text or links to evidence supporting the accuracy rating */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes from the fact-checker */,
  "confidence_level" INTEGER /* Numerical score indicating the confidence level of the accuracy rating */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the accuracy rating was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("accuracy_rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_accuracy_ratings_claim_id FOREIGN KEY ("claim_id") REFERENCES claims ("claim_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_accuracy_ratings_fact_checker_id FOREIGN KEY ("fact_checker_id") REFERENCES fact_checkers ("fact_checker_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "claim_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each assignment */,
  "claim_id" INTEGER /* ID of the claim being assigned */,
  "fact_checker_id" INTEGER /* ID of the fact-checker assigned to the claim */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the assignment (e.g., pending, in progress, completed) */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the assignment was made */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Numerical score indicating the priority of the assignment */,
  "deadline" TEXT /* Deadline for completing the assignment */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the assignment was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_claim_assignments_claim_id FOREIGN KEY ("claim_id") REFERENCES claims ("claim_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_claim_assignments_fact_checker_id FOREIGN KEY ("fact_checker_id") REFERENCES fact_checkers ("fact_checker_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "claim_id" INTEGER /* ID of the claim being analyzed */,
  "accuracy_rating" TEXT /* Accuracy rating of the claim */,
  "trend" TEXT /* Trend of the claim (e.g., increasing, decreasing, stable) */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the analytics entry was created */,
  "confidence_level" INTEGER /* Numerical score indicating the confidence level of the trend */,
  "source_data" TEXT /* Additional data sources used for the analytics */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the analytics entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_claim_id FOREIGN KEY ("claim_id") REFERENCES claims ("claim_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "claim_id" INTEGER /* ID of the claim being reported on */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., summary, analytics) */,
  "report_text" TEXT /* Text of the report */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Brief summary of the report */,
  "recommendations" TEXT /* Recommendations or actions based on the report */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the report was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_claim_id FOREIGN KEY ("claim_id") REFERENCES claims ("claim_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "visualizations" (
  "visualization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visualization */,
  "claim_id" INTEGER /* ID of the claim being visualized */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., chart, graph) */,
  "visualization_data" TEXT /* Data for the visualization */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the visualization was generated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the visualization */,
  "source_data" TEXT /* Additional data sources used for the visualization */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the visualization was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visualization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_visualizations_claim_id FOREIGN KEY ("claim_id") REFERENCES claims ("claim_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, fact-checker, analyst) */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Brief biography of the user */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Specific permissions or access levels of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the user's information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
